MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. BRYAN J. MCCORMACK, Esq. of McCormack Law Firm (Tel: 415-925-5161) for plaintiff;
Atty. SCOTT C. KU, Esq. of Narayan Travelstead P.C. (Tel: 650-403-0150) for defendant The Regents of the University of California;
Atty. MARISSA M. FRANCO, Esq. of Davis Wright Termaine LLP (Tel: 213-633-6800) for defendant Providence Health & Services.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter discussed. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The Regents of the University of California's demurrer to the second amended complaint (SAC) is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 430.41. That statute requires a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a demurrer may be filed and another such meet and confer in the event of a demurrer to any "amended complaint." Here, plaintiff's counsel declares that the meet and confer regarding the SAC was "via email." (McCormack Dec. 1:10-13.) The parties are ordered to comply with the statute and defendants' responses to the second amended complaint are now due by January 6, 2023.

If the parties attempt to re-set this matter for hearing, the parties must first clear the hearing date with the clerk of this department.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
